title: ranunculus abortivus var. nitidus
intro: Ranunculus abortivus var. nitidus, also known as the small-flowered buttercup or shiny-seeded buttercup, is a variety of the Ranunculus abortivus plant, which belongs to the buttercup family and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ranunculus abortivus var. nitidus include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, and to promote the healing of skin wounds. The plant can be prepared as a tincture or infused in oil to create herbal preparations that can be used to treat various health conditions.
